exemplar.title:
To what extent does BHP Group Ltd.âs acquisition of OZ Minerals
align with the company's sustainability goals?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent does BHP Group Ltd.âs acquisition of OZ Minerals
align with the company's sustainability goals?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistent and clear connection of sustainability concept to BHPâs acquisition context
Sustained analytical integration using companyâspecific data (ROCE, production figures, Scope 3)
Regular linkage of Triple Bottom Line and SWOT findings back to the 2030 sustainability pillars
weaknesses: Occasional generic commentary loosely tied to sustainability
Long historical asides (e.g. Doce River) that dilute focus
Operational definition of sustainability could be more concise
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a strong, clear understanding of sustainability with sustained organisational connection (band 4). It falls just short of a fully seamless, comprehensive integration due to minor drifts into generic discussion and occasional over-long digressions, warranting a score of 4/5.

strengths: Three internal documents are clearly identified and directly inform quantitative evidence
Sources supply key metrics (ROCE, production, emissions) that underpin analysis
weaknesses: Overreliance on internal materials with limited external perspective
Depth is unevenâCEO webpage is highly promotional and adds little analytic value
Range of ideas is narrow without third-party or academic sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although three supporting documents are provided and relevant, their depth and balance are limited by exclusive internal sourcing and uneven detail. This aligns most closely with band 2 (general relevance but lack of depth), yielding 2/4.

strengths: Use of standard tools (TBL, SWOT, descriptive statistics) shows basic methodological planning
Applications map to the research question at a surface level
weaknesses: Toolkit remains largely descriptive with limited theoretical sophistication
SWOT is incomplete (missing Opportunities or Threats quadrants)
Statistical application is mentioned but not operationalised with formulas or deeper rationale
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection and application of tools are serviceable but largely descriptive and sometimes incomplete, reflecting band 2 (some tools selected with superficial relevance), so 2/4 is appropriate.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Trade-off analysis between financial gains and decarbonisation spending is compelling
Reputational risks and limitations are acknowledged explicitly
Quantitative evidence (ROCE, production, emissions) is linked to evaluative arguments
weaknesses: Data breadth is limited and some figures lack full triangulation
No sector-average comparisons to benchmark environmental performance
Financial modelling is absent, restricting deeper evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis moves beyond description into mostly effective, integrated evaluation (band 4). It is thorough and sustained, though deeper data triangulation and benchmarking would be needed for the top band, so it earns 4/5.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented
Explicitly and directly answer the research question
Synthesis of financial, environmental, and social findings is coherent
weaknesses: Methodological limitations (e.g., data sparsity) are not discussed
Could strengthen by reflecting on the impact of source bias in conclusions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion provides a clear, evidence-based answer to the research question and synthesises key findings (band 3). Minor additions on methodological constraints would refine it, hence 3/3.

strengths: Logical organization with a clear table of contents and well-labelled sections
Consistent structure facilitates navigation throughout the report
weaknesses: Pagination glitches and full-page text blocks reduce readability
Some sections (e.g., Environmental, Social, Economic) would benefit from sub-headings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report maintains an appropriate and coherent structure overall (band 2), despite minor layout issues, meriting 2/2.

strengths: All required IA elements are present (title page, RQ, word count, bibliography)
Figures and captions are clearly labelled
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting and missing page numbers for figures
Section headings lack corresponding page numbers in the TOC
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all essential requirements (band 2), though uniform formatting and improved citation detail would enhance clarity, justifying 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure with a clear table of contents and well-labelled sections, facilitating reader navigation.  Minor pagination glitches do not disrupt overall coherence, fulfilling the âappropriate structure throughoutâ criterion (band 2).

The three documents are directly relevant and supply most quantitative evidence, but depth is uneven (CEO web page is promotional and offers limited detail).  External articles supplement them, yet these are not counted as formal âsupporting documentsâ.  Overall relevance is good and depth is sufficient, but the range is narrow, so band 3 fits best.
All required IA elements are present: title page with RQ and word count, proper sectioning, bibliography, and separate supporting documents.  Therefore the highest band for presentation is warranted.
Exactly three supporting documents are identified and appended (Annual Report, CEO webpage, FY24 results).  This fits the 3â5 range required for band 3.

Each tool is applied to the research question with reasonable relevance: TBL sections map directly to BHP goals; SWOT critiques internal alignment.  Application sometimes remains descriptive (e.g., listing figures without deeper causal exploration), so effectiveness is adequate rather than highly effective.  Band 3 is therefore justified.

The paper includes several pertinent quantitative points (ROCE, production volumes, emissions changes) drawn from company reports, showing adequate selection and correct use.  Data breadth is limited and some figures are mentioned without full sourcing or triangulation, matching the âadequateâ descriptor in band 3.

Analysis goes beyond description: trade-offs between financial gains and decarbonisation spending are weighed; reputational risks are evaluated; limitations are acknowledged.  Links between evidence and argument are mostly explicit, though depth could be deeper in places (e.g., no financial modelling).  This corresponds to âmostly effective analysis with some integrationâ, band 4.
The research question is explicitly restated and directly answered in the conclusion, meeting the highest expectation for this strand.

The student selects standard but appropriate toolsâTriple Bottom Line, SWOT and basic descriptive statistics.  This represents an adequate, though not extensive or sophisticated, toolkit, aligning with band 3 descriptors.

The conclusion synthesises financial, environmental and social findings and states the overall alignment is âlowâ.  Assertions are coherent with evidence previously discussed, satisfying full consistency, so top band (3) for this strand.

The submission consistently connects arguments to BHP and the OZ Minerals acquisition, using company-specific data (ROCE shift, 39 % production rise, scope 3 figures) and citing internal reports.  This connection is sustained through TBL and SWOT, demonstrating clear understanding of organisational context.  Minor stretchesâfor example long historical digressions (Doce River)âprevent it from being wholly integrated throughout, so a strong (4) rather than comprehensive (5) band is appropriate.

The student places the key concept of sustainability at the centre of the investigation, defining it in the introduction and revisiting economic, environmental and social dimensions in every major section (TBL, SWOT, conclusion).  Analysis repeatedly links findings back to BHPâs stated 2030 sustainability pillars.  However, discussion occasionally drifts into generic commentary on reputation or market positioning that is only loosely anchored to the concept, so integration is strong but not fully seamless; hence band 4 rather than 5.

annotations.comment: Including the word count here is appropriate, but consider relocating it to a footer to maximize space for substantive content.
The table of contents is well-structured with clear headings, facilitating navigation throughout the report.
The methodology paragraph outlines tools but remains high-level; specify which descriptive statistics will be used and why.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and focused, effectively setting the scope for analysis.
The key concept âSustainabilityâ is identified but would benefit from a concise operational definition to anchor subsequent analysis.
Section headings lack corresponding page numbers, which would improve usability for the reader.
The introduction succinctly situates BHPâs 2030 sustainability goals and acquisition context, showing strong link to the research question.
Acknowledging source bias is commendable; consider adding how this might specifically affect each analytical toolâs findings.
Figure 1 is labelled clearly, but the citation lacks publication year and page number for traceability.
The explanation of the Triple Bottom Line is descriptive; deepen by linking each pillar to specific BHP sustainability goals.
Figure 2âs caption merges chart titles; separate captions for ROCE and production charts to improve clarity.
Linking ROCE improvements to reinvestment in sustainability projects shows strong analytical integration.
When discussing ROCE changes, include the formula $$\text{ROCE}=\frac{\text{EBIT}}{\text{Capital Employed}}$$ to demonstrate quantitative understanding.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Environmental analysis uses relevant emission figures but would benefit from comparing BHPâs increases to sector averages.
The text here spans a full page without sub-sections; breaking into Environmental, Social, and Economic sub-headings would improve readability.
The discussion of workforce impacts shows a clear link to community empowerment and inclusivity goals.
Layoffs are referenced but the net change in headcount is unclear; quantify the overall workforce impact.
The SWOT table is missing an Opportunities and Threats quadrant; complete all four sections for balance.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The SWOT commentary effectively links market positioning to long-term sustainability reinvestment.
Proposing green mining technology for OZ operations demonstrates creative application of sustainability tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Identifying the lack of circular business models is insightful; next, propose specific CBM strategies BHP could adopt.
Tie the critique of CBMs back to the research question by explaining how circular initiatives would enhance alignment.
The conclusion restates the research question and summarizes findings concisely, providing a clear endpoint.
The conclusion could be strengthened by acknowledging methodological constraints such as recent data sparsity.
Supporting Document 1 is central but all sources are internal; integrate more external reports to balance perspective.
Citations in the âOther Works Citedâ list display inconsistent formatting; adopt a uniform citation style.
The additional references list would be more accessible if items were ordered alphabetically by author.
